title: aspidosperma sellowii var. genuinum
intro: Aspidosperma sellowii var. genuinum, also known as the "Yellow Aspidosperma" or "Yellow Quebracho", is a plant species native to South America, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its alkaloid content, particularly vincamine and ajmalicine, which have been shown to have vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Aspidosperma sellowii var. genuinum include its potential to improve blood circulation, lower blood pressure, and reduce anxiety and stress. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat cardiovascular and neurological disorders.
